type_legend.invariant: one dominant reading; drill cold as a pure ideogram + revealable hint
type_legend.ambiguous: skeleton has multiple common readings; needs a disambiguating frame
type_legend.derivable: prefix + pronoun or preposition + suffix; mostly free given known affixes

meta.fields: rank=frequency rank; bare=drilled form; niqqud=hidden hint; translit=hint; gloss=meaning (verbs carry person/tense)

readings: et = marks a definite object, precedes it
at = you (f.), stands alone as subject
words.note: Frequency #2; introduced early despite ambiguity — disambiguates trivially.

words.grammar: verb — nif'al present m.sg of ר־א־ה 'see': 'looks / seems / appears'. נראה לי = 'it seems to me'. Same spelling as נִרְאֶה 'we will see' (future).

examples.en: Very nice to meet you, sir.

examples.he: *לפני* שאמות, אני רוצה לעשות אותו הדבר איתך.
examples.en: Before I die, I want to do the same with you.
Sir, we don't have much time.

words.grammar: verb — pa'al present m.sg of ה־ל־ך 'go/walk'; f. הוֹלֶכֶת. הולך + infinitive = 'going to' (near future).

examples.en: What's going to happen to me?

words.grammar: verb — pa'al present m.sg of א־מ־ר 'say'; f. אוֹמֶרֶת. Also 'means': זה אומר = 'that means'.
noun (f.sg) 'a time / occasion'; pl. פְּעָמִים. Also adverbial: פעם = 'once / formerly'.

examples.en: Have you ever heard of this?
She still hasn't gone to work.
Really the life of the party.
The problem is where to hide it.

words.grammar: infinitive of ר־א־ה 'see' (pa'al). רוצה לראות = 'want to see'.

examples.en: How many people is he bringing?

words.grammar: infinitive, pi'el of ד־ב־ר 'speak'; same root as מְדַבֵּר 'speaking', דָּבָר 'thing/word'.

examples.en: I have to talk to you.

examples.he: *היום*, אתה כבר יכול להופיע בפני כולם.
examples.en: Today you can already appear before everyone.

words.grammar: imperative m.sg of בּוֹא 'come' (pa'al); f. בּוֹאִי, pl. בּוֹאוּ. בוא נ... = 'let's...' (בוא נלך 'let's go').

examples.en: Come on, let's get out of here!

words.grammar: verb — pa'al present m.sg of א־ה־ב 'love/like'; f. אוֹהֶבֶת. Covers both 'love' and casual 'like'.
adjective 'other/another' (m.sg); f. אַחֶרֶת. Idioms: אַחַר כָּךְ 'afterwards', אַחַר הַצָּהֳרַיִם 'afternoon'.

examples.he: זה *תמיד* קורה ביום הראשון של המשפט.
examples.en: It always happens on the first day of the trial.
Besides, are you sure it's sabotage?

words.grammar: adverb — 'surely / probably'; colloquial agreement 'sure!'. From ב־ט־ח 'trust' (בִּטָּחוֹן 'security').
verb — pa'al present m.sg of ק־ר־ה 'happen/occur'. מה קורה? = 'what's up?'. Homograph: קוֹרָה 'beam' (noun).
preposition 'after' (time/place); אחרי ש... = 'after (that)...'. Related to אַחֵר 'other', אַחֲרוֹן 'last'.

examples.en: It was just something he said.
Food will be here in a moment.
That's how a man talks, son.
He seems like a reasonable person to me.
I brought you a few things.

words.grammar: verb — hif'il present m.sg of ב־י־ן 'understand'; f. מְבִינָה. Same root as הֵבִין 'understood', בֵּין 'between'.

examples.en: I want to return a favor.

examples.he: *בשביל* יום ראשון זה היה די מסעיר.
examples.en: For a Sunday, it was pretty exciting.

examples.he: *דרך* אגב, הנה משהו שאולי יעניין אותך.
examples.en: By the way, here's something that might interest you.

words.grammar: infinitive of י־ד־ע 'know' (pa'al). רוצה לדעת = 'want to know'; same root as יוֹדֵעַ 'knows', יֶדַע 'knowledge'.

examples.en: Do you really want to know?

words.grammar: 'such / like this' (m.sg); f. כָּזֹאת kazot. = כְּ + זֶה ('like this').

examples.en: I didn't do such a thing!

words.grammar: verb — hitpa'el present m.sg of כ־ו־ן: 'mean / intend'; f. מִתְכַּוֶּנֶת. למה אתה מתכוון? = 'what do you mean?'.
noun (m.sg) 'food'. Same spelling as אוּכַל ukhal 'I'll be able' and אוֹכֵל okhel 'eating' — context decides.

examples.en: Food will be here in a moment.

words.grammar: infinitive of א־מ־ר 'say' (pa'al) — bookish twin of לְהַגִּיד. כְּלוֹמַר = 'that is / i.e.'.

examples.en: What do you want to say?

examples.he: *כאילו*, מה הדבר האחרון שהם חושבים עליו?
examples.en: Like, what's the last thing they think about?
It's hard for me to explain, sir.
Ten years is a long time.

words.grammar: adjective/verb 'dead' / 'dies' (pa'al, root מ־ו־ת); f. מֵתָה. Colloquial: מֵת עַל / מֵת לְ = 'crazy about / dying for'.

examples.en: I'm dying for a cup of tea.
Where do you expect to find him?

words.grammar: verb · present (beinoni) · m.sg · pi'el — or noun 'desert'

examples.en: It didn't go well tonight, did it?

words.grammar: noun (m.sg); f. בַּחוּרָה 'young woman'. Same root as לִבְחֹר 'to choose', בְּחִירוֹת 'elections'.

examples.he: זו הייתה טעות *לתת* לך לחזור הנה.
examples.en: It was a mistake to let you come back here.

words.grammar: adverb 'never' — past tense only, with לא: מעולם לא = 'never (have I)'. Lit. 'from eternity'. (Future 'never' = אַף פַּעַם / לְעוֹלָם לֹא.)

examples.en: The guys are doing nice work.
Or I thought it would be nice.

words.grammar: verb — nif'al present m.sg of מ־צ־א 'find': 'is found / located / present'; f. נִמְצֵאת.

examples.en: Let me get out of here!

words.grammar: verb · present (beinoni) · m.sg · pa'al — or noun 'employee'

examples.en: That's how it sounded this morning.
Or do they think anything at all?

examples.he: אתה לא *מכיר* את הבן אדם בכלל.
examples.en: You don't know the guy at all.

words.grammar: preposition 'without' (formal twin of בְּלִי). ללא ספק = 'without a doubt'.

examples.en: Well, let me tell you something.
It was all a bad dream.

words.grammar: verb · present · m.sg · pi'el — or noun מִשְׁנָה 'Mishna'

examples.en: It doesn't really matter to me.

words.grammar: noun (m.sg); pl. רְחוֹבוֹת. From ר־ח־ב 'wide' — a street as a 'broad way'.

examples.en: I found him in the street.
When did you say it should be?
End of the road, everybody out.
And now she's part of it.
To go on being a man.

words.grammar: verb — pi'el present m.sg of ק־ו־ה 'hope': אני מקווה ש... = 'I hope that...'; f. מְקַוָּה. Homograph: מִקְוֶה mikve (ritual bath).

examples.en: Really the life of the party.
When did something happen to you under the house?

words.grammar: adverb 'alone'; with suffix: לְבַדִּי 'by myself', לְבַדּוֹ 'by himself'. Distinct from מִלְּבַד 'apart from'.
discourse adverb 'actually / in practice / in fact'. From מַעֲשֶׂה 'deed/act' (root ע־ש־ה 'do').

examples.en: And I didn't know if I heard him right.
Little Jonathan ran to the garden in the morning
...for me to stay among the living.

words.grammar: ordinal 'first' (m.sg); f. רִאשׁוֹנָה. From רֹאשׁ 'head'. יוֹם רִאשׁוֹן = Sunday.

examples.en: He was the first to arrive.
I'll have to think about it.

words.grammar: verb — hif'il present m.sg of נ־ג־ע ('reach'): 'arrives' AND 'is deserved': זה מגיע לי = 'I deserve this'; f. מַגִּיעָה.

examples.en: Can we get there in time, sir?
I mean, it's already been two weeks.
In a man or a woman?

words.gloss: concept / idea (אין לי מושג = no idea)

examples.he: לא היה לי שום *מושג* על הכסף.
examples.en: I had no idea about the money.
Little Jonathan ran to the garden in the morning

words.grammar: infinitive, nif'al of ש־א־ר. נִשְׁאַרְתִּי = 'I stayed'.

examples.en: I have no connection to her.

words.grammar: noun (m.sg) 'manner / mode'. בְּאוֹפֶן + adj makes an adverb ('in a … way'); בְּכָל אוֹפֶן = 'in any case / anyway'.

examples.he: אני מנסה לדבר איתך כבר שלושה *ימים*.
examples.en: I've been trying to talk to you for three days.

words.grammar: infinitive, pa'al of ש־א־ל. Also 'to borrow'. Same root as שְׁאֵלָה 'question'.
noun (f.sg) — feminine despite no ־ה ending; pl. עָרִים arim. Homograph: הֵעִיר 'woke (someone)'.

examples.en: There's no law in this city.

words.grammar: noun (m.sg); pl. עִנְיָנִים. Related מְעֻנְיָן 'interested'; distinct from מְעַנְיֵן 'interesting'.
noun (f.sg) — feminine; dual יָדַיִם yadayim 'hands'. Don't confuse with מִיָּד 'immediately' or לְיַד 'next to'.
adverb 'immediately / at once'. Written מ+יד ('from hand') but lexicalized. Don't confuse with the noun יָד 'hand' or לְיַד 'next to'.
pa'al present m.sg of צ־ד־ק 'be right'; f. צוֹדֶקֶת. 'אתה צודק' = 'you're right'. Same root as צֶדֶק 'justice'.
infinitive, pa'al of א־כ־ל. Same root as אוֹכֶל 'food'.
infinitive, pa'al of ע־ז־ב. עֲזֹב! = 'leave it! / forget it!'.

examples.en: We can leave whenever you want.

words.grammar: infinitive, pa'al of ע־צ־ר. Also 'to arrest'; עֲצֹר! = 'stop!'.
discourse connector 'therefore / that's why'; וְלָכֵן = 'and so'. Distinct from לָכֶן 'to you (f.pl)'.

examples.en: You were a bad girl, right?

words.grammar: infinitive, pa'al of ק־ר־א — 'read', 'call out', or 'name (call X)'. Context decides.

examples.en: Don't you know how to read?

words.grammar: infinitive, pi'el of ש־ח־ק. מִשְׂחָק = 'a game'.
infinitive of פ־ג־שׁ 'meet' (pa'al). נָעִים לִפְגֹּשׁ = 'nice to meet'.

examples.en: I lost a baby four months ago.

words.grammar: infinitive, hif'il of ת־ח־ל. הַתְחָלָה = 'a beginning'.
infinitive, pa'al of י־ש־נ. הלך לישון = 'went to sleep'.

examples.en: I've been walking for an hour.
I got there after half an hour.

words.grammar: adverb 'soon' (lit. 'in [the] near'). From ק־ר־ב 'be near'; קָרוֹב = 'close / a relative'.

examples.en: We won't see each other soon.

words.grammar: infinitive, pa'al of ב־ד־ק. בְּדִיקָה = 'a test / check-up'.

examples.en: You have no respect for women.
He had a hard chapter in life.

words.grammar: infinitive, pa'al of שׂ־י־ם. שִׂים לֵב = 'pay attention' (lit. 'put heart').
noun (f.sg); pl. מְכוֹנִיוֹת. From מְכוֹנָה 'machine'. Casual synonym: אוטו.
noun (m.sg) — verbal noun of לְסַפֵּר 'to tell'; same root as סֵפֶר 'book'. pl. סִיפּוּרִים.
adverb 'approximately / roughly' (lit. 'in [the] value of'). From עֵרֶךְ 'value'.
noun — always plural in form (מַיִם), takes plural agreement; no singular.
noun (m.sg); pl. יַמִּים yamim (same spelling as 'days'!). 'sea / ocean'.
'equals / is worth' (m.sg), root ש־ו־ה 'be equal'. שָׁוֶה לְנַסּוֹת = 'worth trying'.
noun (m.sg); pl. מַצָּבִים. מַצַּב רוּחַ (lit. 'state of spirit') = 'mood'.
number 'two' — feminine standalone/counting form; construct שְׁתֵּי. Masculine: שְׁנַיִם / שְׁנֵי.
noun (m.sg) — loanword (no Hebrew root); pl. טֶלֶפוֹנִים.
infinitive, hif'il of א־מ־ן. להאמין לְ = 'believe (someone)'; להאמין בְּ = 'believe in'. Cf. אֱמוּנָה 'faith'.
noun (m.sg) — from שֶׁבַע 'seven' (a week = seven days); pl. שָׁבוּעוֹת. שָׁבוּעַיִם 'two weeks'.
noun (m.sg); pl. כּוֹחוֹת kochot. Final ח sounds 'koach' (furtive patach). Don't confuse with שָׁכַח 'forgot'.
noun 'joy', or adj f. 'glad' (אני שמחה = 'I'm glad', f.; m. שָׂמֵחַ). בְּשִׂמְחָה = 'gladly'.
adverb 'especially' (lit. 'in special'). From מְיוּחָד 'special', root י־ח־ד 'single out'.
adjective 'special' (m.sg); f. מְיוּחֶדֶת. Root י־ח־ד 'single out' — same as בִּמְיוּחָד 'especially'.

examples.en: How about a cup of coffee?

words.grammar: infinitive of ד־א־ג (pa'al). אַל תִּדְאַג = 'don't worry'; לדאוג לְ = 'to take care of'.

examples.en: Let me take care of it.

words.grammar: noun (f.sg); pl. מִילִים milim. Near-homograph מִילָה also = 'circumcision' — context decides.

examples.en: Don't say a word on the phone.

words.grammar: infinitive of ת־פ־ס (pa'al). 'catch / grab'; לתפוס מקום = 'to grab/save a spot'.
noun (f.sg); pl. אֲרָצוֹת. הָאָרֶץ = 'the Land' (Israel) or 'the Earth'; כַּדּוּר הָאָרֶץ = 'the globe'.
discourse adverb 'actually / in fact' (lit. 'in essence'). From עֶצֶם 'essence / bone / object'. Softens or corrects: 'actually, …'.
noun (m.sg); f. כַּלְבָּה; pl. כְּלָבִים klavim.
noun (f.sg); pl. דַּקּוֹת dakot. Same spelling as דַּקָּה 'thin (f.)' — context decides.
adverb 'slowly'. לְאַט-לְאַט = 'gradually / little by little'. Opposite of מַהֵר 'fast'.
noun (f.sg); pl. מְדִינוֹת. From דִּין 'law/judgment' — a realm of jurisdiction.
adverb 'certainly / surely / of course' — a touch more formal than בֶּטַח. From וַדַּאי 'certain'.
noun (m.sg) 'manager'; also pi'el present 'manages' (מנהל את…). f. מְנַהֶלֶת.
noun (m.sg); f. מַלְכָּה 'queen'; pl. מְלָכִים. Same letters as מָלַךְ 'he reigned' (verb).
noun (m.sg). Same letters as the verb פָּחַד 'feared' / מְפַחֵד 'afraid'. פַּחַד מָוֶת = 'mortal terror'.
noun (m.sg); pl. גּוּפוֹת/גּוּפִים. In grammar, גּוּף also = grammatical 'person'.

examples.en: Which movie did you like best?

words.grammar: noun (m.sg) 'light'; pl. אוֹרוֹת orot. Root א־ו־ר; לְהָאִיר 'to light up'.

examples.en: It really is a small world.

words.gloss: to go down / get off / fall (rain)
words.grammar: infinitive of י־ר־ד 'descend' (pa'al). לרדת מ = 'get off/out of'; יוֹרֵד גֶּשֶׁם = 'it's raining'.
adverb 'suddenly'. Colloquial מָה פִּתְאוֹם? = 'no way! / what makes you think that?'. From פֶּתַע 'sudden'.
noun (f.sg); pl. כּוֹסוֹת. כּוֹס תֵּה / קָפֶה = 'a cup of tea / coffee'.

examples.en: I brought you a cup of tea.

words.grammar: noun (m.sg) 'meat / flesh'. Same root as בְּשׂוֹרָה 'news/tidings'.
noun (m.sg); pl. מַפְתְּחוֹת. From פ־ת־ח 'open' — a key opens.
noun (f.sg) 'sun'. Don't confuse with מְשַׁמֵּשׁ 'serves' (same letters, ש-מ-ש).
noun (m.sg); pl. עֵצִים etzim. Means both 'tree' and 'wood'.

words.gloss: long ago / for a long time
words.grammar: adverb 'long ago / for ages' (lit. 'from [a long] time'). From זְמַן 'time'. 'לא ראיתי אותך מזמן' = 'long time no see'.

examples.en: I haven't seen you in a long time.

words.grammar: noun (f.sg); pl. מִיטוֹת. From נ־ט־ה 'incline / lay down'.

examples.en: I'm tired, I'm going to sleep.

words.grammar: the contrary-to-expectation adverb (Aramaic): 'actually / precisely / of all things'. 'דווקא אהבתי' = 'I actually DID like it'; 'דווקא היום?' = 'today of all days?'. Signals a twist against what's expected.
adjective 'official / formal'; בְּאוֹפֶן רִשְׁמִי = 'officially'. From ר־ש־ם 'register / record'.
noun (m.sg) 'rain'; pl. גְּשָׁמִים. יוֹרֵד גֶּשֶׁם = 'it's raining' (rain 'comes down').

examples.en: Can't a guy make a meal?

words.grammar: infinitive, hif'il of צ־ל־ח 'succeed'. הִצְלַחְתִּי = 'I managed/succeeded'; הצלחתי לְ + inf = 'I managed to…'.

examples.en: He wanted to succeed at any cost.

words.gloss: to be wrong / make a mistake
words.grammar: infinitive of ט־ע־ה 'err' (pa'al). טָעִיתִי = 'I was wrong'. Cf. טָעוּת 'mistake'.
adjective 'off / extinguished' (m.sg); f. כְּבוּיָה, pl. כְּבוּיִים. Opposite דָּלוּק 'on'. From כ־ב־ה 'go out'.
